title: Full PWA Setup with Manifest, Service Worker, and Icon Assets

requirements.text: Ensure frontend/index.html includes a complete and valid PWA manifest link, all required Apple PWA meta tags (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style, apple-mobile-web-app-title), a theme-color meta tag, and viewport meta tag. Confirm the manifest.json path is correctly referenced.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: index.html links to /manifest.json via <link rel='manifest'>
Apple PWA meta tags are present and correctly set
theme-color meta tag is present matching the app's theme color
viewport meta tag is present with width=device-width, initial-scale=1

requirements.text: Create a complete frontend/public/manifest.json with all required PWA fields: name, short_name, description, start_url, display (standalone), orientation, background_color, theme_color, icons array covering 72x72, 96x96, 128x128, 152x152, 192x192, 384x384, and 512x512 PNG sizes, categories, and screenshots if possible.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json is valid JSON and passes PWA manifest validation
All icon sizes from 72px to 512px are referenced in the manifest
display is set to 'standalone'
start_url, name, short_name, theme_color, and background_color are all present

requirements.text: Generate and place all required PWA icon PNG files in frontend/public/icons/ for sizes: 72x72, 96x96, 128x128, 152x152, 192x192, 384x384, and 512x512. Also generate a 180x180 apple-touch-icon.png placed at frontend/public/apple-touch-icon.png. Reference these icons correctly in manifest.json and index.html.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: All icon files exist at the specified paths
manifest.json icons array references the correct paths and sizes
index.html includes <link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/apple-touch-icon.png'>
Icons are visually consistent with the CRYONEX sci-fi theme

requirements.text: Rewrite frontend/public/service-worker.js to implement a robust, production-ready service worker with: correct cache versioning, pre-caching of all static assets listed in a manifest, a cache-first strategy for static assets, a network-first strategy for API calls, proper install and activate lifecycle events that clean up old caches, and graceful offline fallback to the cached index.html for navigation requests. Ensure the draft environment bypass logic (skipping caching on draft.caffeine.xyz) is preserved.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: Service worker installs and activates without errors
Static assets are pre-cached on install
Old caches are deleted on activate
Navigation requests fall back to cached index.html when offline
Draft environment bypasses all caching
No console errors related to the service worker on a fresh load

requirements.text: Ensure the service worker is registered correctly in frontend/index.html or the app entry point. The registration should only run in production (not on draft), handle registration errors gracefully, and log success/failure to the console. Use navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js') with the correct scope.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: Service worker registration code is present and runs after page load
Registration is skipped on draft.caffeine.xyz environment
No uncaught errors from failed registration
Browser DevTools Application tab shows the service worker as active

requirements.text: Update frontend/.ic-assets.json to ensure service-worker.js is served with Cache-Control: no-cache, max-age=0 so browsers always fetch the latest version, while all other static assets can use longer cache durations appropriate for a PWA.

quotes: Can u pls make it a PWA that works completely without any problem

acceptanceCriteria: service-worker.js has Cache-Control: no-cache in .ic-assets.json
manifest.json has appropriate cache headers
Icon files have appropriate long-lived cache headers

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Preserve the draft environment cache bypass logic in the service worker and utilities
All icon files must be placed in frontend/public/ or subdirectories thereof
nonGoals: Any changes to backend Motoko logic
Changing the app's visual design or color scheme
